compiled_state.title: merge-to-main.sh: agent uses --version-bump flag -> silently ignored, wrong version type applied
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Agent hallucinated --version-bump=minor instead of using the correct --bump minor flag when calling merge-to-main.sh to perform a minor version bump. The incorrect flag was silently ignored, causing a patch bump instead.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. User requests a minor version bump when merging worktree to main
2. Agent calls: .claude/scripts/dso merge-to-main.sh --version-bump=minor
3. The flag is unrecognized and ignored
4. Patch bump occurs instead of minor bump (e.g., 1.12.46 instead of 1.13.0)

## Expected Behavior
Agent uses --bump minor to trigger a minor version bump. merge-to-main.sh documentation and/or CLAUDE.md Quick Reference should make the correct --bump flag visible and discoverable.

## Actual Behavior
Agent hallucinated --version-bump=minor (likely pattern-matching from other tools). The unrecognized flag was silently ignored. A patch bump was applied instead.

## Root Cause
CLAUDE.md Quick Reference only shows 'merge-to-main.sh [--resume]' with no mention of --bump. The correct flag name --bump is not documented in the agent-visible reference table, making hallucination likely.

## Fix Suggestions
1. Add --bump to CLAUDE.md Quick Reference: 'merge-to-main.sh [--resume] [--bump major|minor|patch]'
2. Make merge-to-main.sh reject unknown arguments with a clear error rather than silently ignoring them

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): Bug not reproducible in current code. Zero occurrences of --version-bump anywhere in repo. All actual callers (sprint, end-session, debug-everything, VERSIONING.md) use --bump correctly. Catchall at merge-to-main.sh:758-760 is NOT silent — emits 'WARNING: Unknown argument' to stderr. Optional defensive alias could be added but low priority.
comments.author: Test
